content.thinking:
Now I have a clear picture:

**Construction sites (emit/synthesize `LoopAuditEvent::PolicyDenial { ... }`):**
1. `crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/agent_loop.rs:273` - the agent loop constructs and emits the event when a tool call is denied by policy
2. `crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/tool_enforcement.rs:92` - `EnforcedAuditSink::emit` synthesizes a new `PolicyDenial` event when it sees a `ToolCallRequested` for a disallowed tool


3. Several locations pattern-match on `PolicyDenial` to read and transform its fields—one mirrors it into a `§7 tool.denied` event, others extract fields for examples and assertions

I'm also tracking down the `AuditSink` implementors to see the full picture of how these events flow through the system.

content.text:
I have everything I need to answer precisely.

## A. PolicyDenial constructors

- `crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/agent_loop.rs`: calls `sink.emit(&LoopAuditEvent::PolicyDenial { ... })` inside the agent loop when the policy layer rejects a tool call during iteration
- `crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/tool_enforcement.rs`: synthesizes a new `LoopAuditEvent::PolicyDenial { ... }` (line 92) inside `EnforcedAuditSink::emit` when it intercepts a `ToolCallRequested` for a tool not in the allowlist — a secondary enforcement layer that catches bypassed loop checks

(The match arm at line 70 of `tool_enforcement.rs` and the `if let` in `examples/tool_allowlist.rs:102` and `examples/v2_runtime_smoke.rs:202` only destructure the event to read fields; they do not construct it.)

---

## B. AuditSink implementors

- `crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/audit/mod.rs`: `NullSink`
- `crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/audit/mod.rs`: `InMemorySink`
- `crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/audit/mod.rs`: `JsonlFileSink`
- `crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/tool_enforcement.rs`: `EnforcedAuditSink`

## A. PolicyDenial constructors

- `crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/agent_loop.rs`: calls `sink.emit(&LoopAuditEvent::PolicyDenial { ... })` inside the agent loop when the policy layer rejects a tool call during iteration
- `crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/tool_enforcement.rs`: synthesizes a new `LoopAuditEvent::PolicyDenial { ... }` (line 92) inside `EnforcedAuditSink::emit` when it intercepts a `ToolCallRequested` for a tool not in the allowlist — a secondary enforcement layer that catches bypassed loop checks

(The match arm at line 70 of `tool_enforcement.rs` and the `if let` in `examples/tool_allowlist.rs:102` and `examples/v2_runtime_smoke.rs:202` only destructure the event to read fields; they do not construct it.)

---

## B. AuditSink implementors

- `crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/audit/mod.rs`: `NullSink`
- `crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/audit/mod.rs`: `InMemorySink`
- `crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/audit/mod.rs`: `JsonlFileSink`
